fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Arkansas Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Arkansas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity arkansas, how to transfer physical therapist license to arkansas, physical therapist compact arkansas, arkansas physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Arkansas Board of Physical Therapy
board.mailing_address: Arkansas State Medical Board, 1 Commerce Way, Suite 302, Little Rock, AR 72202-2070

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Arkansas.

steps.title: Create Account and Start Application
steps.description: Access the ASMB Online Services Portal at https://www.armedicalboard.org/LicenseeLogin/Login.aspx, register as a new user, and select the appropriate Physical Therapist application type (e.g., Initial by Exam or Endorsement).

steps.title: Complete Online Application Form
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the application within the ASMB Online Services Portal, providing comprehensive personal, educational, and professional history details.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your physical therapy program to be sent directly to the Arkansas State Medical Board. If applying by exam, ensure your NPTE scores are submitted by FSBPT. For endorsement, arrange for official verification of licensure from all states where you have held a PT license.

steps.title: Undergo Criminal Background Check (CBC)
steps.description: Follow the specific instructions provided by the Arkansas State Medical Board, which will be accessible through the application portal, to complete the required fingerprint-based criminal background check.

steps.title: Pay Application Fees
steps.description: Submit the initial application fee and any other applicable fees, typically through the ASMB Online Services Portal, to finalize your application submission.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Arkansas physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $100. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Arkansas have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Arkansas participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Arkansas without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Arkansas?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 6 to 8 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Arkansas while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Arkansas
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Arkansas, you must create an account and complete your application through the ASMB Online Services Portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the ASMB Online Services Portal, click on 'New User Registration', and follow the prompts to create an account using your email address before initiating an application.
